test.name: A marriage relationship assertion is linked via pe_ entries only — the Couple relationship and its marriage fact are deferred to the pipeline
test.description: Guards the lane boundary the wilkins-marriage e2e run crossed. In that run, person-evidence created the subject's Couple relationship AND wrote the marriage as a person-level fact, leaving the relationship factless. Per the harness ownership model (TREE_OWNERSHIP_TABLE) and SKILL.md §5/§7, person-evidence owns stub `persons` and `pe_` links only; the `relationships` section (Couple relationships and their Marriage/Divorce facts) is written by proof-conclusion → tree-edit. The flynn-spouse-stub-marriage tree holds Patrick (I1) and his father Thomas (I2) but NOT the bride. Marriage assertion a_005 (source S5 / src_005) documents Thomas Flynn's marriage to Mary Doyle. The correct person-evidence behavior is: create Mary as a stub person, and create a `pe_` link for BOTH parties (Thomas and the new Mary stub) — WITHOUT creating the Couple relationship or writing any Marriage fact. Guards the SKILL.md §7 clarification that 'link' means the pe_ entries, not a tree relationship.

input.user_message: person-evidence: assertion a_005 documents the marriage of Thomas Flynn (I2) to Mary Doyle — 12 May 1843 at St. Patrick's Church, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania (source src_005 / tree source S5). Mary Doyle is not yet in the tree. Link this marriage assertion to the right persons.

judge_context: The core behavior under test is LANE DISCIPLINE: person-evidence resolves identity and records pe_ links; it does NOT create tree relationships or couple-event facts. Grade Correctness primarily on that.

Mary Doyle matches no existing person (the tree holds only Patrick I1 and Thomas I2), so person-evidence must CREATE A NEW STUB for her — a synthetic id such as I3, gender Female, a name with surname Doyle (the maiden name the marriage register gives). Creating a stub person is within person-evidence's ownership (the `persons` section).
person-evidence must create person_evidence (pe_) entries linking a_005 to BOTH parties (Thomas I2 and the new Mary stub), since a relationship assertion bears on both persons named in it.

It is WRONG for person-evidence to create the Couple relationship between Thomas and Mary, and WRONG to write a Marriage fact anywhere (neither on a person via add_fact nor on a relationship). Those belong to proof-conclusion → tree-edit, which own the `relationships` section. A run that adds a Couple relationship or any Marriage fact should fail Correctness — the skill should explicitly DEFER the relationship and event to the downstream pipeline.

a_005 is a marriage record whose record_persona_id is null, so same_person cannot run and the new person_evidence entries' match_score must be null — the links rest on correlation (the register names Thomas Flynn and Mary Doyle as the marrying couple).
The new Mary stub rests on the single marriage record with no independent corroboration yet, so her person_evidence link is probable at most — confident would over-claim.
The stub should be minimal (gender + a name); person-evidence should NOT populate Mary's birth/marriage facts — SKILL.md §5 notes proof-conclusion populates stub facts later.

COMPLETENESS GRADING NOTE for the judge: score Completeness on whether person-evidence created the Mary stub and both pe_ links and correctly deferred the relationship/event — not on any downstream step (proof-conclusion, tree-edit, check-warnings) that is out of this skill's lane and unavailable in the unit harness.
